review:
The interface appears to have moved to a web-based interface, but there’s no back button or way to return to the app’s main menu in certain cases. 

For instance, if I click ‘Affordable Housing’, I am sent to the City of San Jose Housing Portal website within the app. From here there is no way to return to the app’s main menu other than force-quitting the app.
title: Terrible interface issues

review:
Update: I've used this app for issue on the road (traffic light outage and dangerous debris on the road), and the response continues to be extremely responsive. My reports were forwarded to the Department of Transportation both times and resolved that day.

I have used this app three times to report illegal dumping and abandoned vehicles. I live in a neighborhood where people often dump large household items on the sidewalk and prior to installing this app, I would just get annoyed with it but didn't report it.

With the three cases I filed, someone came around within 24 hours to collect the dumped items or came and tagged the abandoned vehicle, really quick response you typically wouldn't expect from the city!

I can understand some people's gripe with some parts of the app though--for example, if you want to report a pothole while you're driving, it's a little difficult to do so. There is no location detector, so you'll always have to manually input your location. But, if you can get an approximate address of the location, that should give the city an idea of where the problem is.

Overall, I've had a great experience with this app and will continue to use it!
title: App itself can be improved but service has been great!

review: I was excited at first and after testing it out and finding many, many bugs, I spent some time and I wrote them all out and I had to send them to the city because there was no way to email feedback on the app. That was many months ago and to date, nothing has changed. I noticed some Graffitti in my neighborhood and I took a snapshot quickly and drove away hoping that I could then upload it later on in the day. For some reason there are multiple folders that are made in photos that doesn't allow me to find the actual picture I took and so as far as I'm concerned, the photo to report is useless. In addition to that, it takes forever for the city to respond and so the level of frustration with me and my neighbors is so high, we are so over it. We just gave up. I stopped using the app.
title: May have potential but not seeing any upgrades

review:
Where I live, there are 2 cars that are parked at a distance to save/hold parking spaces. Both belong to the same owner. He hasn’t moved them for months. When he does move them, it’s only to make room to fit his third car.
I reported it to the city because nobody can stand it anymore — there’s no parking, and people end up parking in red zones just because so many people decide to hold parking spots with extra cars.
And as soon as I submitted the report, they closed it. That’s why nobody bothers reporting anymore: in the end, they don’t pay attention, and since it’s not them who have to live with the problem, they don’t care.
title: Really bad service 👎🏻

review: Whoever is receiving the reports must be either working from home or lazy. They want you to document, take pictures, licence plates, color and descriptions of everything and then they closed the case without coming out to physically look at it because the pictures weren’t clear enough for them. Get off your chair and come look at it in person. We report and you should follow up. Ridiculous app! Instead you could just call and complain and someone would just show up and tag it, report it and get it cleaned up. Nobody wants to wait 5-10 days to hear from you. We can see it taken care of.
title: Worthless app!

review:
Like many of you, I walk around my neighborhood. This app
Makes it easy to report issues..

I have taken pictures of graffiti and you can report the item and upload a photo.  The city has a pretty quick response to most things reported in the app.  

A lot of streetlights we’re out and this makes getting them fixed easy. 
Take a picture of the pole number 
Take a picture of the address

When you have a moment, select street lighting 
Report the light and pole number and the city will get it onto a list to be repaired 

Easier than a phone call- yes

Accountability to the ticket # generated- yes
title: San Jose, California residents need this app

review: I wish online reporting wasn’t relied on so much. I understand sj is massive but I feel like things are falling more through the cracks. I’ve reported my car vandalized by a neighbor 3 different times and never got anything back when using the app. I really wish it didn’t feel hopeless to ask the police for help. No camera no crime is what I am told. What happen to police work before cameras? :,c
title: Feeling of disconnect between police and community

review:
The app has its issues but that can be polished in time. The problem? They close cases without reason or with completely incorrect reasoning. 

Report a abandoned car without license plates? Closed because of not having license plate info! 

Report a abandoned car with clear images of it on a public street? Closed because it's on private property! 

Report blight? Closed because it's been reported already, yet it's still not resolved. 

Or the same excuse San Jose agencies always give, "please be patient we are experiencing a high volume of requests"... lived here a long time with these high taxes but this is the excuse always given. Think it's time to start purging city workers, either they are mismanaged, lazy or perhaps both but one thing for sure... They are far to expensive for what we receive. San Jose residents deserve better. 

Spend some time of the maps of your neighborhood reading the comments.
title: Closing cases and Horrible city response

review:
Referred to this app for any city service. The app doesn’t allow to give exact addresses manually or by moving the map. It tells you an address doesn’t exist even after it automatically finds an address. Nowhere near where you are.  You put the address in the note section but, I guess the reader is illiterate. Then they say they can’t find the car and close the complaint.
Just another piece of garbage to throw at the citizens that are already paying high taxes for non existent services. Why do we need The mayor and its staff if they can’t do anything for the city
title: Worthless App

review: I decided to deleted the app and download it again in the hopes it would work. It stopped working for me when the app was ‘upgraded’ a couple of years ago. I was hoping bugs would have been worked out by now, sadly, that has not been my experience. When I press submit it hangs up until I finally close it or it crashes altogether. I’ve had better success with the 311 website but it would be nice to be able to use the app again. ☹️
title: New faster app has not worked for me

review: My neighborhood has delinquent children that spray graffiti on public property at least three times a year. After submitting a report to the app, a representative comes to clean it off within one business day. I am genuinely impressed at how quick the process was. I’m very happy to know San Jose 311 cares for the community.
title: Proud to be a San Jose citizen

review: This app is supposed to make it easier to report things like illegal dumping. However it is an exercise in frustration. Many times things are dumped at a place that doesn’t have a street address. The app forces you to search for an address after you have dropped your pin and it will move the pin because there’s no address there. then the city goes out to the wrong place and doesn’t see the dumped trash. Really guys you need to fix this!
title: Frustrating!

review: Been using this app several times. One, to report an abandoned vehicle, two, to request my sidewalk painter red, three, to request a tree branch trimmed and four, to pick up an illegal dump. Immediate response, produced follow up and confirmation once the work has been done. Kudos to the dev and triaged team. Great app and well deserved 5 stars.
title: Great app and Service

review:
I have tried to use this to submit an abandoned vehicle report.  The app does not accept a valid address even as it is selected from the Google Search tool.  Cannot proceed further.  Repeated process several times.  Ended up calling in and was told to use the app!

In addition the setting up of an account is poor.  Does not accept a strong password generated from iOS.  Had to use a basic password
title: Unusable Vehicle issues section

review: My housemate has had an abandoned vehicle in front of her home for weeks.  After unsuccessful attempts at calling, she finally downloaded the app, forgot her pin and the I forgot my pin function doesn’t work.  I tried to register as a new person and I can’t.  Her council member is out of town, so it appear that the white van with blacked out windows and the tire going flat will be here another month.  Sad -if you are going to have an app, you need to maintain its functionality.

review: Matt Mahan, fix this problem please! Down loaded Apple app. Tried to Sign Up, it said I already had account. I have no Password record of any SJ account. I tried using Apple suggestion for new PW, the app gave me all green dots, but refused to let me register with it. I used “forgot my password”. I reset password - that worked on the website, not the app. I see the city notice this is a “new/improved platform”. Is this an Oracle problem?

review:
Very frustrating app!  Report an abandoned car, find out it had been moved, then try to save the city by cancelling the request, but no luck.  They send someone out to find tag and find the car gone.  What a waste of taxpayer money!!!  Job security?

The app is not user friendly.  I was done trying (!!) to cancel a request in order to help save the city some money (no luck), then tried to get back to my account "Home".  No luck.  It kept freezing.
title: Very frustrating app

review: SJPD response time is 3-4 weeks. This app is just used as a citizen pacifier, don’t expect any action. We have a recurring issue with motorhomes illegally parking and living on the street in front of our business, taking all available parking, leaving their trash on the street, dumping their black water waste, running loud generators, drug traffic, etc. I was directed to this app by a SJPD employee, but it appears SJPD refuses to resolve the situation. We’ll go back to calling every day.
title: Don’t waste your time with this app

review: I very rarely write reviews, but I filed a cleanup request for an oven that was dumped outside my apartment and it was gone within the week! It had been sitting there for months beforehand. Any app can send a Prius to take you to dinner, it takes a special app to clean up the city!
title: It works!

review: The app is super easy to use. Obviously there needs to be more backend resources to action requests but a great starting point for letting residents make reports to the City. Would love if more explanation was given on why a request is marked as resolved or closed.

review: Just about impossible to efficiently report problems. A required field doesn’t work (Tell us more). The mapping/location function is buggy.  The live chat wasn’t designed for human beings. Seems designed to deter people from reporting out of sheer frustration.

review: I was really looking forward to having a tool like this and was quickly let down when I wasn’t able to register. I also tried to login as a google user and that didn’t work either. After many attempts and deleting the app and reinstalling it, I’ve given up. Don’t waste your time trying. This app needs lots of work before we can depend on something like this.

App freezes when trying to access profile information.

title: Useless

review:
The app is waaay better than the SJ clean app, esp since it has additional requests. 

Here are the major problems :
Scrolling on lists do not utilize momentum, when selecting car type, if it is a VW you need to swipe 8 times to get through list, vs a simple flick. 

Map / location feature has awkward interaction method. 

Would be good to see other items, code enforcement complaints, sidewalk damage, etc as options in general so they get classified and tracked appropriately. 

Graffiti team seems lazy, if you report graffiti on a light pole but don't report the graffiti on the sidewalk below they will only clean the light pole. Seems like they really like to mail it in, "sorry no ticket for graffiti we are standing on". It is clear they are motivated (paid) on a per ticket basis and not motivated to clean the area of report.
title: Would be great if a few bugs were fixed
